## 1. New LLM Versions / Major AI Lab Tools
- 2026-04-02 - [Microsoft announces MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, and MAI-Image-2](https://microsoft.ai/news/today-were-announcing-3-new-world-class-mai-models-available-in-foundry/). Summary: Microsoft launched three in-house models for speech-to-text, speech generation, and image generation in Foundry. Why it matters: Microsoft is deepening its own model stack in core multimodal workloads instead of relying only on third-party labs.
- 2026-04-02 - [Codex now offers more flexible pricing for teams](https://openai.com/index/codex-flexible-pricing-for-teams). Summary: OpenAI added pay-as-you-go pricing for Codex in ChatGPT Business and Enterprise. Why it matters: lowers procurement friction for team adoption and makes Codex easier to trial at smaller scale.
- 2026-04-02 - [Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/workspace/google-vids-updates-lyria-veo/). Summary: Google expanded Google Vids with no-cost creation, editing, and sharing updates tied to its AI video stack. Why it matters: pushes generative video deeper into mainstream workplace software, not just developer or creator tooling.

## 2. FOSS / open-weight LMs & tools
- 2026-04-02 - [Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemma-4/). Summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemma 4 in 2B, 4B, 26B MoE, and 31B variants under Apache 2.0, with multimodal input, long context, and agentic optimization. Why it matters: this materially raises the open-model baseline for deployable, permissively licensed systems.

## 3. Major financial AI company news
- 2026-04-02 - [OpenAI acquires TBPN](https://openai.com/index/openai-acquires-tbpn). Summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN to expand AI-focused media and conversation with builders, businesses, and the wider tech ecosystem. Why it matters: this is a distribution and narrative-control move, not just a product move.

## 4. Major AI data center news
- 2026-04-02 - [Maine Is About to Become the First State to Ban New Data Centers](https://www.wsj.com/us-news/maine-data-center-ban-e768fb18). Summary: Maine is advancing a bill to pause new data centers of 20MW or more until November 2027 over power-demand and grid concerns tied to AI growth. Why it matters: if enacted, it would be the first statewide moratorium of its kind in the U.S. and a clear sign that AI infrastructure expansion is running into local political limits.

## 7. AI Workflows & Tactics
- 2026-04-01 - [The Ultimate AI Catch-Up Guide](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jov9Mn2Q2s8). Summary: the highest-signal workflow is to use AI as an iterative operator, not a one-shot answer engine. Tools/models: any current frontier chat model; optional agent or automation layer after the workflow is stable. Setup: 1) provide raw context first, 2) request a draft, 3) force a critique pass, 4) tighten constraints and iterate, 5) verify against source material, 6) only then automate the repeatable parts. Available now: yes. Why it matters: this is a practical way to reduce hallucinations while getting immediate productivity gains in research, writing, and automation.
